title: Data Description
text: Please note: a full description of these data sources will be provided at a later date.

title: Data Description
text:
Packages used
Please note: this list is currently incomplete and will be updated at a later date
Kyle Walker and Matt Herman Tidycensus: Load US Census Boundary and Attribute Data as ’Tidyverse’ and ’Sf’-Ready Data Frames (2026), https://walker-data.com/tidycensus/.
Greg Freedman Ellis et al. Ipumsr: An r Interface for Downloading, Reading, and Handling IPUMS Data (2026), https://tech.popdata.org/ipumsr/.

title: Data Description
text:
Data Management
High level description: ACS and Census column labels were cleaned to standardize them and prepare them for analysis and place names were changed to a more readable form. The data was subset to only those counties in ETDD’s counties.
Tiger files were used to get the area of each municipality and county. The area was converted into square miles and combined with the census data population to get population density. The data was subset to only those counties in ETDD’s counties.
IPUMS data was subset, the column names were changed, and changes from year to year were calculated and added to the data.
LODES data was combined at the county level and the crosswalk was used to add the correct label for each county.

title: Union
text:
PUT MAP HERE
INTRODUCTION This report is part of a series, which examines statistical data from U.S. Census materials regarding economic and demographic characteristics in the sixteen counties that make up the East Tennessee Development District. The series utilizes data from U.S. Decennial Censuses along with American Community Survey Five-year Estimates as its primary sources. Please see the Data Description Tab for a detailed list of where the data comes from.
Topics that will be discussed in this summary report for Union County are • population growth from 1950 to 2020; • population density; • demographic characteristics in terms of sex, race, and age; • economic characteristics in terms of family income, household income, per capita income, and poverty status of families; • employment classification according to industry; • labor force characteristics in terms of place of work and commuting patterns; • educational level of the adult population; • and housing characteristics in terms of age of the housing stock and type of tenure.

title: Home
text:
This is the ETDD Census Reporting Dashboard for our counties. It is currently not complete.


Hover over “Counties”, then click on the county you want information for
Click on the tab for the category of information you want
Click on the tab for the table and graph you want
Some graphs are interactive- if you hover your mouse over them you will notice controls like zooming in or out. You can hover over data points to get the exact numbers and category label (which are also in the corresponding table). You can click and drag to limit the graph to a smaller part of the overall data. On some graphs, if you click on the color boxes to the side you can show/hide some of the categories in the graph.




Overlapping axis labels
Inconsistency between interactive vs static graphs
Missing accessibility check on drop down
Missing Data Description
Introduction on each dashboard requires rewriting + formatting
Missing map on each dashboard
Missing details on Data Sources
Missing data export option for tables
Raw labels for data in interactive graphs
Some tables and graphs missing data sources





Include more interactivity (Shiny)
Include option for patterns instead of colors on graphs
Include ability to export tables easily
Include District-wide view
